programme.title: CAFÉ DE CGTN
programme.desc: En este espacio, los compañeros de la familia de CGTN en español, intercambian opiniones y debaten de forma casual los temas económicos, sociales y culturales de mayor actualidad en China.

programme.title: EL PROYECTOR
programme.desc: Acérquese a la vida de la China antigua y moderna a través de las producciones dramáticas de CGTN-Español. La combinación perfecta de historia y ficción chinas en la pantalla de su televisor.

programme.title: CGTN NOTICIAS
programme.desc: Es el informativo estelar del canal, con las últimas noticias del acontecer político, social, deportivo y cultural de China y el resto del mundo. Los hechos que constituyen titulares están aquí, en CGTN Noticias.

programme.title: SABOREANDO CHINA
programme.desc: Aprenda a preparar las recetas más populares de China. Nuestro chef le revelará de forma práctica los secretos del arte culinario de esta nación asiática. Conozca los ingredientes más utilizados en la variada cocina regional, los orígenes de los platos e incluso sus beneficios para la salud. En De China a tu cocina, el espectador podrá experimentar a la vez la riqueza tradicional y la variedad gastronómica de China.

programme.title: NIHAO CHINA
programme.desc: Es la clase de chino de CGTN-Español. Las series “Aprendamos juntos pinyin”, “Comunícate en chino” y “Viajando y aprendiendo chino”, abordan diferentes niveles de aprendizaje de la nueva lengua de moda en el mundo, brindando así a los televidentes la posibilidad de ampliar su vocabulario, mejorar su gramática y dominar las frases y expresiones más usuales del idioma con más hablantes en el planeta.

programme.title: DOCUMENTAL
programme.desc: Presentapersonajes y acontecimientos típicos, avances y descubrimientos tecnológicos y científicos, y la civilización social de China.

programme.title: ASÍ ES CHINA
programme.desc: Descubre para el televidente el gran legado cultural e histórico de las distintas regiones y etnias de China. También le acerca a la vida y las costumbres de la gente común y le muestra la evolución del país en aspectos como educación, sociedad y protección del medio ambiente y del patrimonio histórico y natural.

programme.title: HORA INFANTIL
programme.desc: Una selección de las mejores series chinas de dibujos animados, dobladas al español. Hora Infantil es una ventana a través de la cual los niños y adolescentes de los países de habla hispana pueden llegar a comprender mejor qué es y cómo es China.

programme.title: RONDA ARTÍSTICA
programme.desc: Se exhibe la diversidad de cantos y danzas, óperas locales tradicionales, música y artes folklóricas de China.

programme.title: CGTN NOTICIAS
programme.desc: Es el informativo estelar del canal, con las últimas noticias del acontecer político, social, deportivo y cultural de China y el resto del mundo. Los hechos que constituyen titulares están aquí, en CGTN Noticias.

programme.title: CAFÉ DE CGTN
programme.desc: En este espacio, los compañeros de la familia de CGTN en español, intercambian opiniones y debaten de forma casual los temas económicos, sociales y culturales de mayor actualidad en China.

programme.title: CGTN NOTICIAS
programme.desc: Es el informativo estelar del canal, con las últimas noticias del acontecer político, social, deportivo y cultural de China y el resto del mundo. Los hechos que constituyen titulares están aquí, en CGTN Noticias.

programme.title: DOCUMENTAL
programme.desc: Presentapersonajes y acontecimientos típicos, avances y descubrimientos tecnológicos y científicos, y la civilización social de China.

programme.title: EL PROYECTOR
programme.desc: Acérquese a la vida de la China antigua y moderna a través de las producciones dramáticas de CGTN-Español. La combinación perfecta de historia y ficción chinas en la pantalla de su televisor.

programme.title: China Untapped: Nanjing 2
programme.desc: In this episode, we explore the grand old mansions of Nanjing’s merchant families and take a stroll through their exquisite gardens, where Nature is incorporated into the architecture. From centuries-old Qing Dynasty residences to traditional folk handicrafts, join us on a deep dive into Nanjing’s rich cultural heritage.

programme.title: Personalized tours draw more global travelers to Beijing
programme.desc: As China’s visa-free policy continues to attract visitors from around the world, more international tourists are choosing customized experiences in Beijing. Local guides fluent in multiple languages are helping them discover the city in their own way. Reporter Li Qiong joined oneone of the capital’s most popular guides to see how personalized tours are reshaping travel in China.

programme.title: China Untapped: Nanjing 3
programme.desc: Join us in Nanjing’s bustling old quarter, where you can try on silk dresses worth their weight in gold, and see how traditional handicrafts have been given modern twists. We’ll feast on street snacks and local delicacies by the old Confucian Temple, before taking a pleasure cruise along Nanjing’s mother river to round off our exploration of this vibrant and historic city.

programme.title: From plush toys to holograms: The growing world of AI companionship
programme.desc: Artificial intelligence is evolving—from answering our questions to keeping us company. From robotic pets in our neighborhoods to holographic characters that sense our mood, AI companionship is stepping out of science fiction and into everyday life. What does this mean for human relationships and the future of connection itself? Reporter Zhang Hong explores the rise of these new companions.

programme.title: Nyingchi Ep.1
programme.desc: Join Tianran He in Nyingchi, Xizang Autonomous Region. Fed by the Yarlung Zangbo River and abundant rainfall, this lush subtropical region is home to some of Xizan’g most unique scenery. From sacred mountains and ancient monasteries, to cruising along Nyingchi’s mother river, wandering alpine meadows and savoring stone pot chicken – a beloved local delicacy, Tianran uncovers the beauty of Nyingchi.

programme.title: Young Chinese dare to express their culture
programme.desc: A fresh chapter in cultural storytelling is being written by young Chinese around the world. Young Chinese are amplifying tradition through trend, donning hanfu not only in historical lanes but in everyday life, and weaving those visuals into the fabric of social media. Our reporter Ioana Gomoi meets the creators who are turning heritage into a trendy cultural inspiration.

programme.title: Dance of Heroes
programme.desc: In this episode, our host “Uncle Hanzi” travels to Chaonan in Shantou to explore the story of a family devoted to the tradition of Yingge dance. Centered around inheritor Hong Shaohua, the story unfolds through his father’s dedication, his daughter’s passion, and his apprentices’ enthusiasm—showcasing how this centuries-old folk art continues to thrive in the modern world.

programme.title: LIVING THE NEW WAVE China's livestream shopping boom redefines e-commerce
programme.desc: Now, we’re taking you inside one of China’s most dynamic phenomena—the livestream shopping revolution. From the misty tea mountains of Guizhou to Beijing’s glittering jewelry studios, millions tune in daily not just to purchase, but to connect. How did this fusion of entertainment and e-commerce become such a powerful cultural force? And what does it tell us about the future of shopping worldwide? Our reporter Lucy Lyv is here with us.

programme.title: Living Arts of Huizhou
programme.desc: This is Huangshan in eastern China. In this city, you’ll encounter one of China’s most traditional building styles: Hui-style architecture. How does Hui style set this region apart? What’s life like for people living in these houses? With these questions in mind, we’re embarking on a journey to decode Hui-style architecture.

programme.title: INVESTING IN JOY: Inside China's new emotional economy
programme.desc: Today's young Chinese generation are driving a new "emotional economy," spending not just on necessities but on feelings. From trendy toys that offer joy and community, to serene scents that provide a moment of peace – they are investing in personal well-being, reshaping the consumer landscape.

programme.title: Pingyao Hand-Polished Lacquer Art
programme.desc: Pingyao is an old town with a history of over 2,700 years in the north of China, in Shanxi Province. The rich cultural heritage it built up over the centuries eventually gave rise to a very special art—Tuiguang lacquerware. This hand-polished lacquerware, unique to Pingyao, is recognized as one of the four representative lacquerwares of China.

programme.title: The Inner Horizon: China's extreme sports revolution
programme.desc: They say in China, the most exciting frontiers aren't always on a map. A new cultural wave is sweeping the nation, turning vertical rock faces, open skies, and dense forests into new playgrounds. What was once the domain of a handful of adventurers is now a booming lifestyle for millions of young Chinese. I recently ventured out to explore why extreme sports are becoming the new national pastime, and what this reveals about the changing spirit of China.

programme.title: Suzhou Fans
programme.desc: Suzhou, known as the “Venice of China,” is also famous for its traditional crafts. And one of the most elegant and exquisite crafts is Suzhou fans. Celebrated for their exquisite design and fine quality, they’re popularly associated with literati. How come Suzhou gave birth to such a variety of fans? How can we appreciate their beauty in the modern world? In this episode, we are discovering more stories behind Suzhou fans.

programme.title: The beat of the city: Inside China's booming Livehouse scene
programme.desc: From the intimate jazz vibes of Beijing's Blue Note to the electric energy of a Guangzhou livehouse — we dive into China's booming nightlife scene. Discover how these venues are more than just places for a night out — they're cultural hubs where young audiences connect, artists innovate, and the city's heart beats to a brand new rhythm.

programme.title: Miao Silverware: A Shining Epic
programme.desc: In this episode, we explore the fascinating world of the silver-working art of the Miao ethnic group. Join us, and hear the stories and see the works of four remarkable individuals. From a national-level inheritor who is preserving the traditional craft, to an artist infusing personal flair into his designs; from a female silversmith imbuing her work with feminine warmth, to a young artisan inspiring a new generation, we investigate the rich heritage, cultural significance, and evolving potential of Miao silverware.

programme.title: Nyingchi Ep.2
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He continues his journey through Nyingchi, Xizang Autonomous Region. He meets the Lhoba people, China’s smallest ethnic group, who show him their traditional hunting attire. At a sacred lake, Tianran visits an ancient monastery and witnesses the 3000-year-old dance of the Gongbo Tibetans, before venturing to a remote village to experience their their way of life.

programme.title: Dive into the world of foreign talent in China's movie industry
programme.desc: We go behind the scenes of China's fast-evolving film industry with foreign actors making their mark here. From script study to on-set cultural moments, they share how they learn, interpret, and help bring Chinese stories to life. Their journeys highlight not only China's growing appeal and openness but also the vibrant, two-way cultural exchange unfolding on the global stage.

programme.title: Nyingchi Ep.3
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He rounds off his travels through Nyingchi, Xizang Autonomous Region. He journeys into the Yarlung Zangbo Grand Canyon – one of the largest and deepest canyons on Earth, and along the way, tries to catch a glimpse of Mount Namcha Barwa, the easternmost peak of the Himalayas. Instead, he encounters a waterfall said to be a gateway to the afterlife, before finally arriving at the entrance to the canyon.

programme.title: Personalized tours draw more global travelers to Beijing
programme.desc: As China’s visa-free policy continues to attract visitors from around the world, more international tourists are choosing customized experiences in Beijing. Local guides fluent in multiple languages are helping them discover the city in their own way. Reporter Li Qiong joined oneone of the capital’s most popular guides to see how personalized tours are reshaping travel in China.

programme.title: Wenzhou
programme.desc: Tianran He is in Wenzhou, southeast China — a city famed for its self-made millionaires. Here he meets the first Chinese shoemaker to expand overseas, and hears the stories of other local entrepreneurs. Along the way, he samples traditional snacks, visits some of China’s oldest lighthouses, and enjoys an evening of Ou Opera. He also learns the craft of papermaking, before heading into the hills to soak in hot springs and wander across ancient covered bridges.

programme.title: From plush toys to holograms: The growing world of AI companionship
programme.desc: Artificial intelligence is evolving—from answering our questions to keeping us company. From robotic pets in our neighborhoods to holographic characters that sense our mood, AI companionship is stepping out of science fiction and into everyday life. What does this mean for human relationships and the future of connection itself? Reporter Zhang Hong explores the rise of these new companions.

programme.title: Outkirts of Wenzhou
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He explores Wenzhou’s counties. From geological wonders and daring cliffside stunts at the Yandang Mountains, to rafting along the scenic Nanxi River, to harvesting seaweed and shellfish on the mud flats of the Dongtou Islands, Tianran discovers the ingenious ways the people of Wenzhou have transformed the challenges of living between mountains and seas into their greatest strengths.

programme.title: Young Chinese dare to express their culture
programme.desc: A fresh chapter in cultural storytelling is being written by young Chinese around the world. Young Chinese are amplifying tradition through trend, donning hanfu not only in historical lanes but in everyday life, and weaving those visuals into the fabric of social media. Our reporter Ioana Gomoi meets the creators who are turning heritage into a trendy cultural inspiration.

programme.title: A Journey on Water from Wuxi to Yixing
programme.desc: Water has always been the heartbeat of human civilization, nurturing the cities it weaves through. In eastern China, the Grand Canal flows across Wuxi, etching its path through history. From the first drops that carved out its waterways to the thriving metropolis we see today, Wuxi’s story is inseparable from the canal that gave it life. In this episode, we uncover how the waters have shaped Wuxi’s identity. This is the story of a city born of water, where the past and present flow together, crafting the future.

programme.title: LIVING THE NEW WAVE China's livestream shopping boom redefines e-commerce
programme.desc: Now, we’re taking you inside one of China’s most dynamic phenomena—the livestream shopping revolution. From the misty tea mountains of Guizhou to Beijing’s glittering jewelry studios, millions tune in daily not just to purchase, but to connect. How did this fusion of entertainment and e-commerce become such a powerful cultural force? And what does it tell us about the future of shopping worldwide? Our reporter Lucy Lyv is here with us.

programme.title: Chengdu Lacquerware Art
programme.desc: Chengdu lacquerware is unique, thanks to its “three carvings and one engraving” technique. Along with its bold decorative style, this has made it a gem of Chinese lacquer art. Chengdu lacquerware also emphasizes the use of vibrant colors. Pieces are brought to beautiful life by bright red, vivid yellow, emerald green, and other striking hues.

programme.title: INVESTING IN JOY: Inside China's new emotional economy
programme.desc: Today's young Chinese generation are driving a new "emotional economy," spending not just on necessities but on feelings. From trendy toys that offer joy and community, to serene scents that provide a moment of peace – they are investing in personal well-being, reshaping the consumer landscape.

programme.title: Diabolo
programme.desc: In China, the diabolo has a history of over 600 years. From the earliest uniaxial design, it developed to have a double axis. With time, it came to be made in various shapes and from various materials. And its popularity began to spread around the world. In the hands of an expert, a diabolo can create a truly mesmerizing spectacle. So, let’s hear from a professional about the various tricks of his trade.

programme.title: The Inner Horizon: China's extreme sports revolution
programme.desc: They say in China, the most exciting frontiers aren't always on a map. A new cultural wave is sweeping the nation, turning vertical rock faces, open skies, and dense forests into new playgrounds. What was once the domain of a handful of adventurers is now a booming lifestyle for millions of young Chinese. I recently ventured out to explore why extreme sports are becoming the new national pastime, and what this reveals about the changing spirit of China.

programme.title: Jingdezhen Handmade Porcelain
programme.desc: Porcelain has shaped the culture of the whole world. The wisdom behind each of 72 procedures has been refined from generation to generation. The times may change, some techniques may change, but what remains the same is the burning flame of the kiln that keeps setting so many hearts on fire for this ancient craft.

programme.title: The beat of the city: Inside China's booming Livehouse scene
programme.desc: From the intimate jazz vibes of Beijing's Blue Note to the electric energy of a Guangzhou livehouse — we dive into China's booming nightlife scene. Discover how these venues are more than just places for a night out — they're cultural hubs where young audiences connect, artists innovate, and the city's heart beats to a brand new rhythm.

programme.title: Mount Wutai, Embracing the Wonders of Nature
programme.desc: Mount Wutai, in the northeastern part of Shanxi Province, is one of the Four Great Buddhist Mountains of China. It is a magnet for tourists, who are drawn here not only by its profound Buddhist cultural heritage, but also by the spectacular natural scenery. Mount Wutai is noted for its unique topography, marked by five flattened peaks. Hence the name "Wutai," or “Five Terraces.” The five, each with its own characteristics and distinct shape, are Wanghai East Peak, Guayue West Peak, Jinxiu South Peak, Yedou North Peak, and Cuiyan Central Peak. Soaring into the clouds, together they resemble a magnificent work crafted by nature.

programme.title: INVESTING IN JOY: Inside China's new emotional economy
programme.desc: Today's young Chinese generation are driving a new "emotional economy," spending not just on necessities but on feelings. From trendy toys that offer joy and community, to serene scents that provide a moment of peace – they are investing in personal well-being, reshaping the consumer landscape.

programme.title: Diabolo
programme.desc: In China, the diabolo has a history of over 600 years. From the earliest uniaxial design, it developed to have a double axis. With time, it came to be made in various shapes and from various materials. And its popularity began to spread around the world. In the hands of an expert, a diabolo can create a truly mesmerizing spectacle. So, let’s hear from a professional about the various tricks of his trade.

programme.title: The Inner Horizon: China's extreme sports revolution
programme.desc: They say in China, the most exciting frontiers aren't always on a map. A new cultural wave is sweeping the nation, turning vertical rock faces, open skies, and dense forests into new playgrounds. What was once the domain of a handful of adventurers is now a booming lifestyle for millions of young Chinese. I recently ventured out to explore why extreme sports are becoming the new national pastime, and what this reveals about the changing spirit of China.

programme.title: Jingdezhen Handmade Porcelain
programme.desc: Porcelain has shaped the culture of the whole world. The wisdom behind each of 72 procedures has been refined from generation to generation. The times may change, some techniques may change, but what remains the same is the burning flame of the kiln that keeps setting so many hearts on fire for this ancient craft.

programme.title: The beat of the city: Inside China's booming Livehouse scene
programme.desc: From the intimate jazz vibes of Beijing's Blue Note to the electric energy of a Guangzhou livehouse — we dive into China's booming nightlife scene. Discover how these venues are more than just places for a night out — they're cultural hubs where young audiences connect, artists innovate, and the city's heart beats to a brand new rhythm.

programme.title: Mount Wutai, Embracing the Wonders of Nature
programme.desc: Mount Wutai, in the northeastern part of Shanxi Province, is one of the Four Great Buddhist Mountains of China. It is a magnet for tourists, who are drawn here not only by its profound Buddhist cultural heritage, but also by the spectacular natural scenery. Mount Wutai is noted for its unique topography, marked by five flattened peaks. Hence the name "Wutai," or “Five Terraces.” The five, each with its own characteristics and distinct shape, are Wanghai East Peak, Guayue West Peak, Jinxiu South Peak, Yedou North Peak, and Cuiyan Central Peak. Soaring into the clouds, together they resemble a magnificent work crafted by nature.

programme.title: Nyingchi Ep.2
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He continues his journey through Nyingchi, Xizang Autonomous Region. He meets the Lhoba people, China’s smallest ethnic group, who show him their traditional hunting attire. At a sacred lake, Tianran visits an ancient monastery and witnesses the 3000-year-old dance of the Gongbo Tibetans, before venturing to a remote village to experience their their way of life.

programme.title: Dive into the world of foreign talent in China's movie industry
programme.desc: We go behind the scenes of China's fast-evolving film industry with foreign actors making their mark here. From script study to on-set cultural moments, they share how they learn, interpret, and help bring Chinese stories to life. Their journeys highlight not only China's growing appeal and openness but also the vibrant, two-way cultural exchange unfolding on the global stage.

programme.title: Nyingchi Ep.3
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He rounds off his travels through Nyingchi, Xizang Autonomous Region. He journeys into the Yarlung Zangbo Grand Canyon – one of the largest and deepest canyons on Earth, and along the way, tries to catch a glimpse of Mount Namcha Barwa, the easternmost peak of the Himalayas. Instead, he encounters a waterfall said to be a gateway to the afterlife, before finally arriving at the entrance to the canyon.

programme.title: Nyingchi Ep.3
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He rounds off his travels through Nyingchi, Xizang Autonomous Region. He journeys into the Yarlung Zangbo Grand Canyon – one of the largest and deepest canyons on Earth, and along the way, tries to catch a glimpse of Mount Namcha Barwa, the easternmost peak of the Himalayas. Instead, he encounters a waterfall said to be a gateway to the afterlife, before finally arriving at the entrance to the canyon.

programme.title: Personalized tours draw more global travelers to Beijing
programme.desc: As China’s visa-free policy continues to attract visitors from around the world, more international tourists are choosing customized experiences in Beijing. Local guides fluent in multiple languages are helping them discover the city in their own way. Reporter Li Qiong joined oneone of the capital’s most popular guides to see how personalized tours are reshaping travel in China.

programme.title: Wenzhou
programme.desc: Tianran He is in Wenzhou, southeast China — a city famed for its self-made millionaires. Here he meets the first Chinese shoemaker to expand overseas, and hears the stories of other local entrepreneurs. Along the way, he samples traditional snacks, visits some of China’s oldest lighthouses, and enjoys an evening of Ou Opera. He also learns the craft of papermaking, before heading into the hills to soak in hot springs and wander across ancient covered bridges.

programme.title: From plush toys to holograms: The growing world of AI companionship
programme.desc: Artificial intelligence is evolving—from answering our questions to keeping us company. From robotic pets in our neighborhoods to holographic characters that sense our mood, AI companionship is stepping out of science fiction and into everyday life. What does this mean for human relationships and the future of connection itself? Reporter Zhang Hong explores the rise of these new companions.

programme.title: Outkirts of Wenzhou
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He explores Wenzhou’s counties. From geological wonders and daring cliffside stunts at the Yandang Mountains, to rafting along the scenic Nanxi River, to harvesting seaweed and shellfish on the mud flats of the Dongtou Islands, Tianran discovers the ingenious ways the people of Wenzhou have transformed the challenges of living between mountains and seas into their greatest strengths.

programme.title: Young Chinese dare to express their culture
programme.desc: A fresh chapter in cultural storytelling is being written by young Chinese around the world. Young Chinese are amplifying tradition through trend, donning hanfu not only in historical lanes but in everyday life, and weaving those visuals into the fabric of social media. Our reporter Ioana Gomoi meets the creators who are turning heritage into a trendy cultural inspiration.

programme.title: A Journey on Water from Wuxi to Yixing
programme.desc: Water has always been the heartbeat of human civilization, nurturing the cities it weaves through. In eastern China, the Grand Canal flows across Wuxi, etching its path through history. From the first drops that carved out its waterways to the thriving metropolis we see today, Wuxi’s story is inseparable from the canal that gave it life. In this episode, we uncover how the waters have shaped Wuxi’s identity. This is the story of a city born of water, where the past and present flow together, crafting the future.

programme.title: LIVING THE NEW WAVE China's livestream shopping boom redefines e-commerce
programme.desc: Now, we’re taking you inside one of China’s most dynamic phenomena—the livestream shopping revolution. From the misty tea mountains of Guizhou to Beijing’s glittering jewelry studios, millions tune in daily not just to purchase, but to connect. How did this fusion of entertainment and e-commerce become such a powerful cultural force? And what does it tell us about the future of shopping worldwide? Our reporter Lucy Lyv is here with us.

programme.title: Chengdu Lacquerware Art
programme.desc: Chengdu lacquerware is unique, thanks to its “three carvings and one engraving” technique. Along with its bold decorative style, this has made it a gem of Chinese lacquer art. Chengdu lacquerware also emphasizes the use of vibrant colors. Pieces are brought to beautiful life by bright red, vivid yellow, emerald green, and other striking hues.

programme.title: INVESTING IN JOY: Inside China's new emotional economy
programme.desc: Today's young Chinese generation are driving a new "emotional economy," spending not just on necessities but on feelings. From trendy toys that offer joy and community, to serene scents that provide a moment of peace – they are investing in personal well-being, reshaping the consumer landscape.

programme.title: Diabolo
programme.desc: In China, the diabolo has a history of over 600 years. From the earliest uniaxial design, it developed to have a double axis. With time, it came to be made in various shapes and from various materials. And its popularity began to spread around the world. In the hands of an expert, a diabolo can create a truly mesmerizing spectacle. So, let’s hear from a professional about the various tricks of his trade.

programme.title: The Inner Horizon: China's extreme sports revolution
programme.desc: They say in China, the most exciting frontiers aren't always on a map. A new cultural wave is sweeping the nation, turning vertical rock faces, open skies, and dense forests into new playgrounds. What was once the domain of a handful of adventurers is now a booming lifestyle for millions of young Chinese. I recently ventured out to explore why extreme sports are becoming the new national pastime, and what this reveals about the changing spirit of China.

programme.title: Jingdezhen Handmade Porcelain

The times may change, some techniques may change, but what remains the same is the burning flame of the kiln that keeps setting so many hearts on fire for this ancient craft.

programme.title: The beat of the city: Inside China's booming Livehouse scene
programme.desc: From the intimate jazz vibes of Beijing's Blue Note to the electric energy of a Guangzhou livehouse — we dive into China's booming nightlife scene. Discover how these venues are more than just places for a night out — they're cultural hubs where young audiences connect, artists innovate, and the city's heart beats to a brand new rhythm.

programme.title: Mount Wutai, Embracing the Wonders of Nature
programme.desc: Mount Wutai, in the northeastern part of Shanxi Province, is one of the Four Great Buddhist Mountains of China. It is a magnet for tourists, who are drawn here not only by its profound Buddhist cultural heritage, but also by the spectacular natural scenery. Mount Wutai is noted for its unique topography, marked by five flattened peaks. Hence the name "Wutai," or “Five Terraces.” The five, each with its own characteristics and distinct shape, are Wanghai East Peak, Guayue West Peak, Jinxiu South Peak, Yedou North Peak, and Cuiyan Central Peak. Soaring into the clouds, together they resemble a magnificent work crafted by nature.

programme.title: Three Gorges of Chongqing
programme.desc: In this episode, through the new fashion of five-star cruise tour on the Three Gorges, with comfortable and convenient high-end experiences, we take the audience to travel along the river, multi-dimensionally show the most spectacular and beautiful landscape gallery along the Yangtze River, feel the unique charm of canyons, rapids and cultural relics on both banks, and convey the new charm of Yangtze River cultural tourism.

programme.title: Horses in art
programme.desc: 2026 is the Year of the Horse, but what does the animal represent in Chinese culture? Join us as we follow the horse through art: How a sculptor captures the horse's beauty? A Peking Opera performer reveals how to become horse and rider on stage. Xu Beihong's iconic ink paintings and the spirit they inspired across a nation. Ethnic Mongolian dance, expressing the freedom and joy of riding This Year of the Horse: be bold. Pick up the reins, find your herd and charge ahead.

programme.title: My Little Cricket Fighter
programme.desc: In this episode, we explore a time-honored form of entertainment in China that has existed for thousands of years – cricket fighting. Music, Chinese chess, calligraphy and painting, and raising flowers, birds, fish and insects are known as the Eight Traditional Pastimes of the Chinese people. Raising insects may be the last on the list, but it is a pastime unique to China. Though these small creatures are tiny, the culture surrounding them has been nurtured by the Chinese people over thousands of years, embodying the unique cultural psychology, folk customs and aesthetic values of the Chinese nation.

programme.title: Inner Mongolia’s Frozen Wonderland
programme.desc: In this episode, we feature the Winter Nadam Fair and winter sports in Hulunbuir, Inner Mongolia. By late December, the Hulunbuir Grassland is blanketed in ice and snow. Even in the bitter cold of around 30 degrees below zero, the grassland is alive with a festive bustle.

programme.title: Saddling up for new adventures with bold neon and traditional ink
programme.desc: This Lunar New Year, horses are set to steal the show as the Chinese zodiac migrates over to a new animal. But for one Beijing artist, born under this very sign, painting these trusty steeds is more than just a tradition – it’s an expression of freedom. Her work fuses explosive neon colors with bold ink lines. So, what drives her to create these galloping beasts?

programme.title: Exploring the Long Winter
programme.desc: In this episode, we travel to Jingpo Lake, Xuexiang and Yabuli in Mudanjiang, showcasing the unique winter ice fishing festival, the fairytale-like scenery of Xuexiang, and its world-class ski resorts.

programme.title: YEAR OF THE HORSE Why horses in British and Chinese art still speak to the Year of the Horse today
programme.desc: What does an iconic British racehorse have in common with Chinese ink paintings? Quite a lot, actually. This Year of the Horse, we’re looking at how artists across cultures have captured freedom, movement, and spirit.

programme.title: Exploring the Folklore of Chongqing
programme.desc: Exploring the Folklore of Chongqing delves into the historical origins and cultural background of Fengdu Ghost City, and also features the Zhongyuan Festival celebrations and ancestor-worshipping rituals held here. These activities reflect the local people’s positive and optimistic outlook on life and death, as well as the great importance they attach to their ancestors, families and family traditions.

programme.title: Awakening Lion-1
programme.desc: Xingshi, or literally “awakened lion,” is a name for the southern style of the lion dance. Historically, it evolved from a court dance in the Tang Dynasty almost 1,200 years ago. With the southward migration of the population from the central plains, lion dance culture was introduced to the south. In the Ming Dynasty around 400 years ago, xingshi appeared in Guangdong and was then widely spread throughout southern China and even overseas. Lion dance has become a very important part of Chinese culture.

programme.title: Awakening Lion-2
programme.desc: Xingshi, or literally “awakened lion,” is a name for the southern style of the lion dance. Historically, it evolved from a court dance in the Tang Dynasty almost 1,200 years ago. programme.title: Rhythms of the Forest: A Sound of Li Instruments
programme.desc: Li bamboo and wood instruments offer a journey into the unique sounds of Hainan's Li ethnic group. Explore the ancient craftsmanship and musical heritage, from the resonant tones of bamboo flutes to the percussive rhythms of wooden drums. Experience the natural melodies that tell the stories of the Li people.

programme.title: Forged in Legend: A Glimpse of Longquan Blades
programme.desc: Longquan swords, legendary blades from China, embody centuries of masterful craftsmanship. Forged with unique folding techniques and a distinctive star-patterned finish, these swords are renowned for their sharpness, resilience, and artistic value. Discover the rich history and cultural significance of these revered symbols of Chinese heritage.

programme.title: Tales in Light and Shadow
programme.desc: Beijing shadow puppets bring ancient Chinese tales to life with intricately crafted leather figures. Behind an illuminated screen, these colorful silhouettes dance, enacting epic stories and folklore accompanied by traditional music. Discover this captivating performing art, a cherished part of China's rich cultural tapestry and a mesmerizing visual spectacle.

programme.title: YEAR OF THE HORSE Zodiac Girls’ New Year Heights
programme.desc: This zodiac year, the ancient idiom "MaDaoChengGong" promises that success will gallop right to your door. Our culture host, Julian Waghann, saddled up to meet some young equestrians whose personal quests might just teach us all how to leap into our own lucky year! Take a look.

programme.title: Prince Avalanche
programme.desc: Alvin and Lance leave the city to repaint traffic lines and develop an unlikely friendship.

programme.title: Fay Grim
programme.desc: A single mother from Queens becomes unwittingly embroiled in international espionage.

programme.title: Riders of Justice
programme.desc: Recently deployed Markus returns home to care for his daughter after his wife is killed in an accident. But, Markus begins to suspect his wife was murdered and embarks on a mission of revenge.

programme.title: Bronson
programme.desc: In 1974, a misguided 19 year old named Michael Peterson decided he wanted to make a name for himself and so, he attempted to rob a post office.

programme.title: Four Lions
programme.desc: A group of British jihadists push their abstract dreams of glory to the breaking point.

programme.title: Main Street
programme.desc: MAIN STREET is a contemporary drama about residents of a small Southern city whose lives are changed by the arrival of a stranger with a controversial plan to save their decaying hometown.

programme.title: Humpday
programme.desc: It's been a decade since Ben (Duplass) and Andrew (Leonard) were the bad boys of their college campus. Ben has settled down and found a job, wife, and home.

programme.title: Barry Munday
programme.desc: Barry is s missing his family jewels. To make matters worse, he learns he's facing a paternity lawsuit.

programme.title: 2 Days In New York
programme.desc: When Marion's family visits, she's unaware that her boyfriend and familay will make up for an explosive mix.

programme.title: Alan Partridge
programme.desc: Steve Coogan reprises his most popular role as the iconic Alan Partridge, the famous local radio DJ and one time talk show host.

programme.title: The Protector 2
programme.desc: When Boss Suchart is murdered, all evidence points to KHAM (Tony Jaa).

programme.title: Sushi Girl
programme.desc: Fish is released from jail and the men he protected celebrate with dinner.

programme.title: Results
programme.desc: Recently divorced, newly rich, and utterly miserable, Danny [Kevin Corrigan] would seem to be the perfect test subject for a definitive look at the relationship between money and happiness.

programme.title: Results
programme.desc: Recently divorced, newly rich, and utterly miserable, Danny [Kevin Corrigan] would seem to be the perfect test subject for a definitive look at the relationship between money and happiness.

programme.title: Frank
programme.desc: Frank is genius rock musician who never removes his giant fake head. Things get rocky between his bandmates Clara and Jon, who is convinced they’re meant for stardom.

programme.title: Best Man Down
programme.desc: The best man dies at the wedding and the bride and groom end up on an adventure planning his funeral.

programme.title: Broken English
programme.desc: A thirty-something meets a Frenchman who shows her just what an amazing place the world can truly be.

programme.title: The Good Heart
programme.desc: Brian Cox stars as Jacques, the curmudgeonly owner of a gritty New York dive bar that serves as home to a motley assortment of professional drinkers.

programme.title: Please Stand By
programme.desc: Wendy is a brilliant young woman with autism. She sneaks out of her group home to submit her script on-time for a screenplay competition not letting anything stop her from achieving her goals.

programme.title: Italian Studies
programme.desc: After a writer (Academy Award®-nominee Vanessa Kirby) loses her memory, she finds herself on the streets of New York City. She connects with a group of teenagers — in conversations both real and imagined — searching for a way home.

programme.title: Frontera
programme.desc: Miguel crosses the Arizona-Mexican border illegally and is accused of murdering the wife of a former sheriff. His pregnant wife gets involved with Mexican smugglers trying to help him.

programme.title: Blackthorn
programme.desc: Sam Shepard is Butch Cassidy in this western about the legendary outlaw's return from exile.

programme.title: The Life Before Her Eyes
programme.desc: The Life Before Her Eyes is an intense and visually evocative drama about the loss of youth, investigating how a single moment in time can define an entire life.

programme.title: Force Majeure
programme.desc: A wickedly funny psychodrama of a model Swedish family—Tomas, his wife and their children— on a skiing holiday. An avalanche suddenly bears down on them. Tomas makes a decision that leaves him struggling to reclaim his role as family patriarch.

programme.title: Tyrel
programme.desc: Tyrel, a sole black man, attends an all-white weekend of drunken bro debauchery on a birthday trip.

programme.title: Night Catches Us
programme.desc: An ex-black panther returns home to find himself right back into the rivalries and love affair he left behind.

programme.title: The Two Faces of January
programme.desc: This thriller follows Chester MacFarland (Viggo Mortensen) and his wife Colette (Kirsten Dunst) as they befriend a guide (Oscar Isaac) on a European trip. This friendship takes a turn when it morphs into a love triangle full of envy, and murder.

programme.title: The Falls: Covenant of Grace
programme.desc: This third and final film of the Falls trilogy revisits former Mormon missionaries Chris and RJ, six years after they first fell in love and were disciplined for it, as they formulate a plan to be together at long last.

programme.title: Play the Devil
programme.desc: Set against the backdrop of Trinidad and Tobago's mystical Carnival, a gifted and struggling young man becomes the object of intrigue for an older, well-meaning businessman until their worlds collide.

programme.title: Lessons From the Bible

programme.title: Superlibro

programme.title: The Truth About AI (It's NOT What You Think!)
programme.desc: AI has started to feel loud, urgent, or impossible to keep up with, this episode is for you. In this episode of AI for Everyone, Joel Comm helps you step out of the noise and rethink what AI is actually for. No tech lingo. No panic. No pressure to master tools or chase updates. Instead, this episode focuses on a simple, human idea: AI should carry some of the mental load — not add more to it.

programme.title: AI Produced Bad Crypto!
programme.desc: We asked AI to be the show producer this week by picking the top 10 stories in crypto — and to our surprise, it actually did a pretty great job. Joel and Travis react to a packed lineup of stories including Kraken securing a Fed master account, Nasdaq and Kraken teaming up on tokenized stocks, the ongoing disappointment around the U.S. Bitcoin reserve, the Trump meme coin circus getting another pump, stablecoins quietly taking over the rails of global finance, and Bitcoin outperforming gold during an actual war. Along the way, they talk about why stablecoins may be one of the biggest long-term crypto stories, how banks are fighting to slow down change, why tokenized assets keep getting closer to the mainstream, and whether AI might actually be better than humans at pulling together a useful crypto news rundown. It’s fast, topical, opinionated, and loaded with the usual Bad Crypto mix of market takes, jokes, sidebars, and chaos.

programme.title: The Call of the Plateau
programme.desc: In Qinghai's Nianbaoyuze mountain range, 16 local herders—15 of them women—work tirelessly to protect endangered black-necked cranes and the wetlands they call home, even as the dangers of climate change threaten to undo their efforts.

programme.title: Bird's-eye China : Shanghai
programme.desc: Balancing delicately on an alluvial plain and the Yangtze estuary of China’s east coast, much of Shanghai-literally meaning “on the sea”-sits at an average sea-level elevation of about 4 meters. Bisecting the city is the Huangpu River, dividing it into Puxi (west bank) and Pudong (east bank), where you’ll find its famous international airport. Join us as on an aerial cruise as we journey upstream towards one of the world’s most recognizable skylines.

programme.title: Living by Chagan Lake Ep.1
programme.desc: This documentary brings us to Chagan Lake, where ecological protection, the intangible cultural heritage of winter fishing, and local livelihoods paint a striking portrait of harmony between humans and nature.

programme.title: Bird's-eye China : Shanghai
programme.desc: Balancing delicately on an alluvial plain and the Yangtze estuary of China’s east coast, much of Shanghai-literally meaning “on the sea”-sits at an average sea-level elevation of about 4 meters. Bisecting the city is the Huangpu River, dividing it into Puxi (west bank) and Pudong (east bank), where you’ll find its famous international airport. Join us as on an aerial cruise as we journey upstream towards one of the world’s most recognizable skylines.

programme.title: Treasures Along the Great Wall of China Ep.1
programme.desc: Reviewing the Great Wall’s construction history, military value, cultural connotation, and contemporary protection status, this documentary visits the remains of the Great Wall in different sections. programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: Smithy's party has proven to be entertaining and everyone is enjoying themselves. Meanwhile, during the festivities, Bludgy, the city's most notorious thief is seen sneaking upstairs, drawing the attention of others. Finn and Hogar's mission hangs in the balance...

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: Finn and Hogar are tied up below Smithy's tavern along with a few others. Bludgy is about to sell off the crystal rod for a life changing amount ofgold, while Arkie is "retrieving" something important from his workshop.

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: The party, now in possession of the CRYSTAL ROD, has fled from the thieves in the Sunken City through the underground tunnels. Once outside, they discover the Queen's forces are invading from the sea, bombarding the city. They quickly slip out beyond the walls, north of the city... but to go where?

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: The party has been caught trying to escape and with them, the Artifact. Eventually they wake up shackled to a cart, and surrounded by guards on their way back into the occupied Sunken City… at the mercy of the Queen's Forces.

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: "The party has adjusted to their new lives the best ways they know, how but they are eager to escape. Follow the party as they continue working their 9 to 5 jobs with only one lunch break and four 15 minute coffee breaks fun by the evil Queen’s forces."

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: "The Party is in action, and so is the party. Our would be heroes are posing as entertainment to get aboard the rooks ship and steal back the rod before they can escape. Will they succeed?"

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: "Our Party, aka Blackwater Bandits, have earned their name stealing back the rod from the Rook. Now its time for one more performance... the disappearing act. Will they succeed in this mid-season finale?"

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: The Players awake with no memories and must quickly work together to navigate a dangerous dungeon. The extra twist? They have no idea they are playing their actual characters!

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: The party has passed the trial! With acceptance into the city of Anwar, their new goal is recovering those stolen memories...

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: Tragedy has struck the party, a member has fallen. Adding to their strife, they now find themselves in yet another tough situation below Anwar.

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: Hurtling down the water passageway on the journey to The Dark, the party's boat takes a beating. Luckily for them, this means their captor The Lovely Lute takes a beating as well. Now they must try to continue their mission for the Crystal Rod.

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: Who better to go poking around in goblin tunnels than the only members of the party who will fit inside?! But what Arky and Thistle find in the warrens is not what they expected…

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: The party finds the dwarven city of Iron Stone. Unfortunately, it is under siege by strange magical creatures. And what has become of that dangerous assassin, the Lovely Lute?

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: Coming upon the lava-fueled dwarven forge, the party begins to craft anti-magical emerald-imbued weapons. That is, until the whole building begins to shake...

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: Iron Stone is saved! As thanks, the party receives a bounty of magical crystals and a powerful new iron suit for Arky. Now they head for Rock Bottom, a duegar dwarf city beneath a waterfall in the dark Underground.

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: Reaching Pelion Darrus, the Party finds it under devastating attack from above and below. One hero makes an unexpected journey, dragging the party into a surprising decision in this weeks episode!

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: The Audio Dungeon crew begin their adventure in the world of Andaria. Finndelar (Tom) the wizard, and Hogar (Dan) the ranger, have just finished their two month journey, and have reached their destination; The Sunken City. Finn's mission is to retrieve a magical device created in part by a local craftsman: Arkie Fint. Hogar, an ally of Finn, was hired to guide and protect him. When they arrive, they find themselves in the midst of a large celebration, as well as a mystery regarding their mission...

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: Finndelar (Tom) and Hogar (Dan) have arrived in the Sunken City and are now searching for the magical device they have journeyed so far for. After meeting the craftsman Arkie Fint, they travel to Smithy's tavern in hope of finding a clue to their mystery...

programme.title: The Celestial Wars
programme.desc: Smithy's party has proven to be entertaining and everyone is enjoying themselves. Meanwhile, during the festivities, Bludgy, the city's most notorious thief is seen sneaking upstairs, drawing the attention of others. Finn and Hogar's mission hangs in the balance...

programme.title: Love at the Christmas Table
programme.desc: Family friends Sam and Kat spend every Christmas Eve at the Children's Table. They grow up together, sharing the highs and lows of young adulthood. And at thirty, Sam realizes that Kat is the one... but he's afraid that the past will get in the way.

programme.title: A Brief Encounter
programme.desc: Meeting a stranger in a railway station, a woman is tempted to cheat on her husband.

programme.title: The Maltese Falcon
programme.desc: San Francisco private detective Sam Spade takes on a case that involves him with three eccentric criminals, a gorgeous liar and their quest for a priceless statuette, with the stakes rising after his partner is murdered.

programme.title: Blonde Fury
programme.desc: Cindy, an American FBI agent, travels to Hong Kong to investigate a newspaper editor, Ronny Dak, who is suspected of printing counterfeit money using the newspaper’s presses. The American teams up with a rival reporter and her friend Yu, an undercover law enforcer. Cindy’s investigation takes a sharp turn, however, when Yu’s father, the prosecuting lawyer in the counterfeiting case, is kidnapped.

programme.title: Plan Z
programme.desc: One man has a plan for a zombie outbreak, when one occurs it will take all his will to stick to it and all of his courage to change it.

programme.desc: San Francisco private detective Sam Spade takes on a case that involves him with three eccentric criminals, a gorgeous liar and their quest for a priceless statuette, with the stakes rising after his partner is murdered.

programme.title: Blonde Fury
programme.desc: Cindy, an American FBI agent, travels to Hong Kong to investigate a newspaper editor, Ronny Dak, who is suspected of printing counterfeit money using the newspaper’s presses. The American teams up with a rival reporter and her friend Yu, an undercover law enforcer. Cindy’s investigation takes a sharp turn, however, when Yu’s father, the prosecuting lawyer in the counterfeiting case, is kidnapped.
